campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they remembered K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council approved an updated beekeeping ordinance, discussed a state bill about transit-oriented development (with three council members stepping aside due to conflicts of interest), and reviewed a feasibility study about expanding homeless services across the West Valley region.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial - police department's first working dog
Beekeeping ordinance update approved
Senate Bill 79 transit-oriented development hearing
West Valley homeless services regional task force recommended
Fiscal year 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting on March 31, 2026, to review and approve the yearly work plans for five city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and historic preservation. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the coming fiscal year. The council also discussed a legal matter in a closed private session.
keyTopics: Approval of FY 2026-27 work plans for five city commissions
Parks and Recreation Commission priorities for next year
Heritage Preservation and Library/Public Art initiatives
Traffic Safety Commission goals and projects
Planning Commission agenda for 2026-27

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving previous meeting notes and the city's financial report. They also adopted policies on weed control completion, updated council procedures, approved special flag raisings for Pride and Juneteenth, and discussed plans for affordable housing and transportation.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New affordable housing (BMR) unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Housing Element implementation schedule
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority presentation

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and move forward on housing and development projects. They also finalized plans for a new residential development called Solana Project and outlined health and senior services programs for the year.
keyTopics: Approved final development map for Solana Project residential tract
Reviewed city's January 2026 financial and investment reports
Assessed progress on the General Plan and housing element goals
Confirmed 2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
Updated meeting minute procedures and format

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and made changes to the agenda items scheduled for April 7 and April 14, deciding which items needed specific time slots and checking legal notice requirements. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for April 7 council meeting
Prepared draft agenda for April 14 council meeting
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics they wanted to raise. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could voice concerns or suggestions
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Committee and staff updates
Schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings
Contact Recreation Division at 650-903-6331 for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects. The commission discussed and approved the previous month's meeting notes, and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and services for local residents.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding available for housing and community development projects
Review of applications from organizations seeking CDBG (Community Development Block Grant) money
Approval of February 25, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new programs. They discussed offering amnesty to businesses that haven't had proper licenses, reaching out to shopping centers to participate in city initiatives, and creating campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally. The council also explored ways to support small businesses and startups through incentives and partnerships with private companies.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping unlicensed businesses get compliant
Shopping center outreach - getting retail locations involved in city programs
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local campaign - promoting local businesses to residents
Public-Private Partnerships - using private companies to sponsor city events
Economic incentives for startups and small retail - making it easier/cheaper to start a business in Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026 to review matters related to the city's historical sites and landmarks. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and had the option to submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline: noon on meeting day
Remote participation via Zoom or phone

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine administrative meeting on March 17, 2026, to handle housekeeping items like approving previous meeting minutes and setting up the schedule for future meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a wellness conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment to an economic committee.
keyTopics: Approval of previous council meeting minutes
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
New school district representative confirmed for Economic Development role
City Council calendar and upcoming meeting schedule established
Council members' requested agenda items reviewed

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including an official recognition of Women's History Month. The council also approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city buildings as emergency shelters during disasters, and authorized a $93,383 yearly purchase of email security software (Mimecast) for three years.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster shelter agreement for city facilities
Email security software contract ($280,149 total over 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
